key_insight: A fully open-weight multilingual pipeline can turn unstructured news text into signed, temporal political-elite knowledge graphs with high extraction accuracy.
engineering_relevance: The framework shows how scalable entity-resolution and constrained relation extraction can automate large, cross-lingual network mapping from raw text, enabling reproducible computational social science at scale.
method: Span-based NER detects entities in multilingual news corpora.
A three-stage linking cascade resolves mentions to Wikidata identifiers.
An ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model with guided decoding extracts directed, signed relations and builds temporal knowledge graphs.
evidence_signal: A spot-check against a 3,491-relation gold standard reported 68.2% strict correctness and 93.7% lenient textual correctness.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust entity linking and ontology coverage, so extraction errors can propagate into the final signed network.
paper_title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
